nl_query: For each question paper, show the paper title, total marks, number of leaf questions, number of answer key rubrics defined, number of preparers, approval status, and the name of the user who approved it.
failed_sql:
SELECT qp.title, qp.total_marks, COUNT(CASE WHEN q.max_marks IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) AS num_leaf_questions, COUNT(DISTINCT akr.question_id) AS num_answer_key_rubrics, COUNT(DISTINCT ak_preparer.answer_key_id) AS num_preparers, ak.approval_status, app_user.display_name
FROM   question_paper qp
JOIN   board b ON b.qp_id = qp.id
JOIN   evaluation_attempt ea ON ea.board_id = b.id
JOIN   script_assignment sa ON sa.assignment_id = ea.id
JOIN   faculty_cache fc ON fc.id = sa.faculty_cache_id
JOIN   answer_key ak ON ak.qp_id = qp.id AND ak.status = 'APPROVED'
JOIN   answer_key_rubric akr ON akr.answer_key_id = ak.id
JOIN   answer_key_preparer ak_preparer ON ak_preparer.answer_key_id = ak.id
JOIN   app_user ON app_user.id = ak.approved_by
GROUP BY qp.title, qp.total_marks, fc.id, ak.approval_status, app_user.display_name
error: Validation failed (schema): Column 'sa.assignment_id' does not exist. The Postgres planner suggests 'ea.assignment_id' — use that, or verify the column belongs to the qualifying table in your FROM/JOIN clauses.
